steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Word-in-context vocabulary. The blank describes how being featured in the book affected a business, and the next clause explains the cause: inclusion "suggested that a business was an important part of the community." That signals a positive, beneficial result.

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: Because the feature signaled importance in the community, being featured was a benefit — a word meaning "giving an advantage" fits.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'risky': implies danger or downside, but the sentence describes a positive outcome (being seen as important). Contradicts context. ✗
  • Choice B — 'misleading': would mean the feature gave a false impression, but the passage treats the implication of importance as accurate, not deceptive. ✗
  • Choice D — 'unremarkable': means not worth notice, the opposite of the "important part of the community" signal the feature conveyed. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
